metadata.title:
VALLEY FLOOD


SUFFERERS SUE THE CITY

= These things don't happen .
. under the Des Moines plan. .
q 80 . . q y q . 80 90

pages.raw_text:
With their residence filled with
gravel, sand and mud. windows
broken, foundations destroyed, gar
den ruined, and the yard cut into
gullies by torrents from an over
flowed sewer which drove them
from their beds at night to seek a
resting place elsewhere, Ernest
Hess and wife have brought suit
against the city of Spokane for
$4,000 damages.


The plaintiffs are 10 and II years
of age, respectively. and live In
Peaceful valley at the intersection
of Riverside avenue and Oak street,
their four lots situated ON the
sloping hill on the lower side OF
Riverside.


On July %, during heavy rain.
the sewer drain of both Oak street
and Riverside avenue blocked in
the street Immediately in front OF
the plaintiffs house. and the water
overlooked the curb, washing
through his property.


The plaintiff states that his
house was almost filled to the cell
ing with debris and that 1t Is per-
manently ruined. He says he has
contracted sickness from the ex-
posture In leaving the house the
night of the flood. Mr.. Hess states
that his claim for damages filed
with the city was disallowed.


This is the ancient complaint The
Press called attention to time and
again. The city street department
always promised relief and did
nothing.


1t is difficult to see what defense
the city can have In this case. The
people of Peaceful valley were de-
liberately left exposed to an made
quate sewer of which city officials
had full knowledge.
